description.content: To make Tyk installations more portable, the Export API enables you to export key configuration objects required to back-up and re-deploy a basic Tyk Pro installation.
The organisation object is the most fundamental object in a Tyk setup, all other ownership properties hang off the relationship between an organisation and its APIs, Policies and API Tokens.

response.name: Organisation exported successfully.

The import API enables you to add Organisations, APIs and Policies back into a Tyk installation while retaining their base IDs so that they work together.
The organisation object is the most fundamental object in a Tyk setup, all other ownership properties hang off the relationship between an organisation and it s APIs, Policies and API Tokens.

The Dashboard SSO API allows you to implement custom authentication schemes for the Dashboard and Portal. Our Tyk Identity Broker (TIB) internally also uses this API.
The Dashboard exposes the /admin/sso Dashboard API which allows you to generate a temporary authentication token, valid for 60 seconds.
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

For Tyk On-Premises installations only, the Dashboard Admin API has two endpoints and is used to set up and provision a Tyk Dashboard instance without the command line.

In order to use the Dashboard Admin API, you'll need to get the `admin_secret` value from your Tyk Dashboard configurations.

The secret you set should then be sent along as a header with each Dashboard Admin API Request in order for it to be successful:

```
admin-auth: <your-secret>
```
